handoff.purpose: Complete project context and session handoff for onboarding a new developer to Business Glu

project_summary.name: Business Glu
project_summary.description: All-in-one workforce management SaaS platform for deskless and frontline workers. Replaces disconnected scheduling, time-tracking, chat, HR, and training tools with one platform.
project_summary.target_users: Small-to-medium businesses with field, retail, hospitality, healthcare, construction, cleaning, and other on-the-go teams.
project_summary.business_model: Multi-tenant SaaS — Free / Pro / Enterprise tiers (pricing not yet implemented).

tech_stack.routing: Inertia.js (server-driven SPA — NO separate REST API for web app)
tech_stack.styling: Tailwind CSS (brand color #495B67, fonts: Montserrat headings / Lato body)
tech_stack.file_storage: Laravel Storage (local dev → AWS S3 prod planned)

key_architecture_decisions: Inertia.js means NO separate REST API. Controllers return Inertia::render('Page', $data) which renders React components directly.
Multi-tenancy uses stancl/tenancy. Each company gets its own database. The tenant_id column is a string referencing tenants.id.
Two layouts: AdminLayout.tsx (manager/admin dashboard) and UserLayout.tsx (employee app). Admins toggle via ViewSwitchController.
RBAC with 3 roles: super_admin, owner, member. Permissions are checked via role-based middleware and frontend conditionals.
Central database holds: users, tenants, domains, tenant_users. Per-tenant databases hold everything else.

production_data_state.notes: All feed data was seeded via FeedSeeder and TemplateSeeder. Both tenants have identical dummy data.

phase_1_foundation: Authentication (registration, login, password reset, email verification)
phase_2_operations: Time Clock (clock in/out, break tracking, GPS, manual entries, admin review)
Scheduling (shift builder, drag-and-drop, recurring shifts, swap requests, availability)
Forms (form builder, submissions, PDF export, conditional logic, Kanban view)
Quick Tasks (CRUD, assignments, priorities, due dates, Kanban board)
phase_3_communications: Team Chat (1:1 and group messaging, file sharing, reply threads, read receipts, polling-based real-time)
Updates Feed (rich post builder, templates, audience targeting, media uploads, reactions, comments, analytics)
Unified Feed (social timeline aggregating Updates + Events)
Directory (employee directory with search, filters, profile cards)
Surveys & Polls (survey builder, response collection, analytics)
Events (event creation, calendar view, RSVP, reminders)
Help Desk (ticket system, assignments, SLA tracking, categories)
phase_4_hr_people: Courses & Quizzes (course builder, lesson management, quiz engine, progress tracking)
Documents (file management, permissions, version history, categories)
Time Off (request/approve workflow, balances, policy management, calendar)
Recognition & Rewards (peer recognition, badges, points, leaderboard)
Employee Timeline (activity history, milestones, performance notes)
Org Chart (visual hierarchy, interactive navigation, department views)
Digital Employee ID (mobile ID cards, custom fields, instant issuance)
Analytics & Reporting (cross-module dashboard — team, operations, training, communications)
Activity Log & Audit Trail (immutable action history, filters, expandable details)

features_not_yet_built: REST API for mobile app / external integrations

recent_session_changes.summary: Documentation, UI polish, bug fixes, seeding, and deployment

changes.description: Created docs/developer-overview.md (371 lines). Updated docs/project-overview.md and docs/feature-breakdown.md to cover the new Unified Feed feature.
Created TemplateSeeder with 15 realistic update templates (Company Announcement, Policy Update, Safety Alert, Office Closure, Weekly Newsletter, etc.).
Made TemplateSeeder dynamic — auto-discovers tenants instead of hardcoded IDs. Needed because production has different user IDs than local.
Fixed template preview cards in Updates.tsx — tightened phone frame (260x480), fixed empty space below previews.
Fixed mobile preview — merged status bar into app header (eliminated hairline seam between two same-color divs).

Mobile preview UI polish: orange gradient h-16→h-10, reaction icons w-3 h-3 with individual counts. Fixed reaction icons showing as words ('like','love') instead of SVG icons by adding reactionIconMap in UserFeed.tsx. Updated FeedSeeder to use correct SVG icon names.

Fixed 500 error on production: FeedController::home() was using 'assigned_to' column on tasks table, but tasks use a pivot table 'task_user' via assignees() relationship. Changed to whereHas('assignees', ...).
Made FeedSeeder dynamic — auto-discovers tenants and users, skips tenants that already have data. Ran on production to seed both tenants.

known_issues_and_gotchas.explanation: This is expected — tables already exist. It's not a real error. Only run migrations when there are new migration files.

Legacy seeded data stored reactions as words (like, love, celebrate). The UI sends SVG icon names (hand-thumb-up, heart, party-popper). A reactionIconMap in UserFeed.tsx handles both formats. The FeedSeeder now uses the correct SVG names. Production data was fixed.

known_issues_and_gotchas.issue: Tasks use pivot table, not assigned_to column
known_issues_and_gotchas.explanation: The tasks table does NOT have an assigned_to column. Tasks are assigned via the task_user pivot table and the assignees() relationship. Any query filtering tasks by user must use whereHas('assignees', ...).
Local dev uses SQLite, production uses MySQL. Some queries may behave differently. Always test on production after deployment.

The FeedSeeder skips tenants that already have updates (to prevent duplicates on re-run). To re-seed a tenant, you must first delete its existing updates, comments, reactions, reads, audiences, events, and RSVPs manually via tinker.

deployment_quick_reference.full_guide: See docs/deployment.md for the complete 786-line deployment guide including initial server setup, SSL, Nginx config, supervisor, cron, and troubleshooting.

local_dev_quick_start.notes: Local uses SQLite by default. The .env.example should have DB_CONNECTION=sqlite. Create the database file with: touch database/database.sqlite

files.description: Comprehensive developer onboarding — architecture, setup, conventions, feature list, codebase tour (371 lines). START HERE.
High-level project vision, architecture diagram, feature status, and roadmap (507 lines).
Detailed breakdown of every feature with implementation notes.
Full production deployment guide — server setup, Nginx, SSL, supervisor, cron, troubleshooting (786 lines).

backend.controllers: app/Http/Controllers/ — One controller per feature (UpdateController, EventController, FeedController, etc.)
seeders: database/seeders/FeedSeeder.php — 10 updates + 6 events with comments/reactions per tenant

frontend.pages: resources/js/Pages/ — React page components organized by feature

backend: Controllers are thin — business logic in models or service classes where possible
tenant_id is always a string, not an integer
All tenant-scoped models have a tenant_id column
frontend: Tailwind utility classes — no custom CSS files
Brand color: #495B67 (used as 'brand' in Tailwind config)
Consistent spacing: p-6 for card padding
SVG icons via a custom Icon component (no emoji in UI)
Montserrat for headings, Lato for body text
git: Main branch only (no feature branches currently)
All changes committed and pushed before deployment

recommended_first_steps_for_new_dev: 1. Read docs/developer-overview.md (most important — gives full context)
2. Read docs/project-overview.md for the big picture
3. Clone the repo and get it running locally (see local_dev_quick_start above)
4. Log in as owner@demo.com and explore both admin and employee views
5. Read docs/deployment.md if you'll be deploying to production
6. Browse the routes files (routes/web.php, routes/admin.php, routes/user.php) to understand the URL structure
7. Look at a feature end-to-end: e.g., Updates → UpdateController.php → Updates.tsx → Update model
